content.text:
I am making a full shopify website forking the dawn theme - this is the PRD so far from Gemini Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlines the development of a high-end, Shopify-based platform for property services. Based on your provided mockups and the aesthetic of Homemove, the site will move away from a traditional "e-commerce store" look toward a "SaaS-style Digital Agency" feel.

PRD: Modern Property Services Marketplace (Shopify)
1. Project Overview
Objective: To provide a seamless, high-conversion platform for landlords and sellers to purchase legal, marketing, and survey services.

Target Audience: DIY Landlords, Property Investors, and Homeowners looking for "unbundled" estate agency services.

Visual Identity: Dark-mode aesthetic with high-contrast neon accents (Cyan & Purple), glassmorphism, and smooth scroll animations.

2. Visual & Aesthetic Requirements
Based on your mockups and the Homemove reference, the UI must follow these strict guidelines:

Color Palette:

Primary Background: Deep Navy/Charcoal (#0B1120).

Accents: Electric Cyan (#00F2FF) for primary CTAs and Purple/Magenta (#7000FF) for secondary highlights/gradients.

Surface Colors: Semi-transparent "Glass" cards (Background blur: 10px).

Typography: Bold sans-serif (e.g., Poppins or Inter).

Key "Homemove" Features to Replicate:

Reveal-on-scroll animations: Elements fading and sliding upward as the user scrolls.

Pulse Effects: Subtle glowing borders on high-value "Buy Now" cards.

Interactive Hover States: Cards that lift or glow when hovered.

3. Detailed Service Catalog (Product Logic)
Since Shopify is built for physical goods, each property service requires a custom Metadata Schema to collect property details.

Service	Requirement Logic	Key Features
RICS Level 2 Survey	Address collection + Scheduling	Calendar integration (e.g., Calendly or Tipo app).
Auction Legal Pack	Document intensive	File upload field for current deeds/titles.
Property Title	Instant digital delivery	Automated PDF delivery via Digital Downloads app.
Professional Photography	Field Service	Zip code validator to ensure coverage before checkout.
Rent Increase / Tenancy	Legal Compliance	Form-fill logic to generate the specific notice/agreement.
Portal Boosting	Variable Duration	Options for 7-day, 14-day, or "Until Sold" boosts.
4. Functional Requirements & User Flow
A. The "High-Conversion" Homepage
Hero Section: A "Choose your path" interactive toggle: I am Selling vs. I am Renting.

Trust Bar: Floating Trustpilot / Google Reviews widget (as seen in your WhatsApp mockup).

Service Grid: Use the "Glassmorphism" card style. Each card shows:

Service Icon (Custom neon line-art).

"Starting from £X" price tag.

"Add to Bundle" button (to encourage multi-service purchases).

B. Custom Product Pages (PDP)
Standard Shopify templates won't work here. We need:

Property Address Picker: A Google Maps API integrated field on the product page to verify the property address before adding to cart.

Dynamic Pricing: E.g., Survey prices that adjust based on the number of bedrooms or property value.

Process Timeline: A visual "Step 1, 2, 3" showing what happens after they pay (e.g., 1. Order Placed -> 2. Agent Assigned -> 3. Delivery in 48h).

C. The Checkout & Post-Purchase
Upsell Logic: If "Professional Photography" is in the cart, trigger a pop-up for "Portal Boosting" at a 10% discount.

Customer Dashboard: A custom "Order Status" page where users can download their RICS reports or Title Deeds once uploaded by your team.

5. Technical Stack (Shopify Ecosystem)
Theme Foundation: Dawn (for speed) but heavily customized with Liquid/CSS for the dark-mode aesthetic.

Essential Apps:

Matrixify: For managing complex service data.

Globo Product Options: To add the "Property Address" and "Number of Bedrooms" fields to products.

Search & Discovery (Shopify): For the "Recommended Services" logic.

Klaviyo: For automated "Service Reminders" (e.g., "Your Tenancy Agreement expires in 30 days").

6. Development Roadmap
Phase 1: Brand & Core Architecture (Weeks 1-2)
Set up Shopify store with the dark-mode CSS framework.

Configure the "Service" product template (removing shipping/weight logic).

Phase 2: Functional Customization (Weeks 3-4)
Integrate Google Address Autocomplete.

Build the "Bundle & Save" logic for multiple property services.

Implement the "Homemove" style animations (Framer Motion or CSS Keyframes).

Phase 3: Launch & SEO (Week 5)
SEO landing pages for "Property Title Deeds UK" and "Cheap RICS Level 2 Survey."

Payment Gateway setup (Stripe/Apple Pay/Klarna).

7. Next Steps
Would you like me to generate the specific "Product Description" copy for each of these services, optimized for both SEO and the high-end tone of your site?

You said
ensure it is also optimised for mobile too and the delivery wont be able to be automated yet

Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) is an evolved version of the previous draft, now specifically focused on mobile-first architecture and a manual fulfillment workflow as per your requirements.

PRD: Digital Property Services Platform (Shopify)
1. Project Objective
To create a high-end, mobile-optimized marketplace for property services that mimics the sleek, animated feel of Homemove while managing professional service delivery through a manual, high-touch fulfillment process.

2. Visual Identity & Animations (The "Homemove" Style)
To replicate the "cool" features found in your reference sites, the frontend will implement the following:

Glassmorphism & Orbs: Use "Floating Gradient Orbs" (Coral, Blue, and Purple) with high blur (80px) and low opacity (0.05-0.15) as background decorative elements to create depth.

Micro-Animations:

Reveal-on-Scroll: Sections should use a fadeIn animation (sliding 20px upward) as the user scrolls to them.

Pulse Effects: Backgrounds should have a subtle pulseBgMist animation to feel "alive".

Typography: Utilize the Poppins font family for a clean, modern, and professional aesthetic.

3. Mobile-First UX Requirements
The site must be "thumb-friendly" and optimized for high-speed mobile browsing:

Sticky Mobile Navigation: A transparent header that becomes opaque on scroll, containing only the Logo, a Search Icon, and a Hamburger Menu.

Vertical Service "Stacks": On mobile, services should not be in a grid but in large, clickable cards with a 1:1 aspect ratio and vibrant neon borders.

The "Bottom Sheet" Cart: Instead of a full-page cart, use a sliding "bottom sheet" for quick service additions, minimizing friction for mobile users.

Haptic-Style Buttons: Large, full-width "Buy Now" buttons (minimum height 54px) with rounded corners and high-contrast gradients.

4. Manual Fulfillment & Operational Workflow
Since delivery is not automated, the Shopify backend must be configured to capture data at the point of sale and manage the fulfillment pipeline manually.

A. The "Information Capture" Phase (Product Page)
Because you need to perform the service manually, you must collect specific property data before the customer checks out.

Tool: Use a "Product Options" app (e.g., Globo or HulkApps).

Fields required per service:

Full Property Address (Google Maps Autocomplete integration).

Access Details (e.g., "Is there a key safe?", "Tenant contact name").

Urgency Level (Dropdown: Standard vs. Fast Track).

B. Post-Purchase Workflow (The Manual Loop)
Order Received: Customer receives a "Welcome & Next Steps" email (via Klaviyo) explaining that an expert is now reviewing their case.

Internal Processing: Admin receives the order with all custom property details.

Manual Updates: Admin uses Shopify "Order Tags" (e.g., In-Progress, Awaiting-Surveyor, Drafting-Pack) to track status.

Delivery: Once the service (e.g., Auction Legal Pack) is ready, the Admin manually uploads the PDF/Link to the customer's "Order History" and sends a "Your Service is Ready" notification.

5. Granular Service Breakdown
Service	Specific Mobile UI Requirement	Manual Fulfillment Task
Auction Legal Pack	Progress bar showing "Drafting -> Legal Review -> Done".	Solicitor/Admin compiles deeds and legal docs into a single PDF.
RICS Level 2 Survey	Calendar date picker (optimized for mobile touch).	Admin coordinates with the surveyor and property occupier for access.
Outlays	Simple "Add to Cart" with clear cost breakdown list.	Admin verifies local authority fees and pays them on behalf of the client.
Photography	Image gallery showing "Before vs. After" (Swipeable slider).	Admin books a photographer; manual upload of edited photos via Dropbox link.
Property Title	"Instant Request" button.	Admin manually pulls the title from the Land Registry.
Rent Increase / Tenancy	Step-by-step form (One question per screen).	Admin drafts the legal notice and sends for digital signature.
Portal Boosting	"Select Platform" toggles (Rightmove/Zoopla).	Admin manually applies the "Featured" or "Premium" credit on the portal.
6. Technical Stack Recommendations
E-commerce Engine: Shopify (using a highly customized Dawn theme).

Data Capture: Globo Product Options (to collect property addresses).

Customer Communication: Klaviyo (to manage the manual delivery updates and "thank you" flows).

Mobile Performance: Cloudflare for image optimization and Lazy Loading for the "Homemove" animations.

7. Immediate Next Steps
Select a Lead Developer: Someone proficient in "Liquid" (Shopify's language) and GSAP/Framer Motion for the animations.

Map out the "Welcome Email": Draft the copy for the email customers get immediately after paying to manage their expectations regarding manual delivery times.

Setup Property Form: Define exactly what information is needed for the "Auction Legal Pack" versus a "Photography Service."

You said
for the auction pack we wont be able to have a progress bar and they cant pick a date for rics

Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) is built for a high-end, Shopify-based property services platform. It incorporates the "cool" features and animations from Homemove, the functional structure of Let Property, and the specific manual fulfillment constraints you requested.

PRD: Premium Property Services Marketplace
1. Visual & Design Identity (Mobile-First)
The site will use a "SaaS-style Digital Agency" aesthetic rather than a traditional e-commerce look.

A. Color Palette & UI (Based on Mockups)
Primary Background: Deep Space Navy/Black (#0B1120).

Accents: Electric Cyan (#00F2FF) for primary buttons and Neon Purple (#7000FF) for gradients and secondary highlights.

Surface: "Glassmorphism" cards with 15% opacity white backgrounds and 10px backdrop-blur.

Typography: Poppins (Semi-bold for headers, Regular for body) to match the professional yet modern feel.

B. "Homemove" Animations & Features
Reveal-on-Scroll: Elements must slide up 20px and fade in as the user scrolls, using the fadeIn keyframes found in the Homemove code.

Floating Orbs: Decorative, blurred circles (Cyan and Purple) will move slowly in the background to provide depth.

Pulse Bg Mist: Subtle background pulses on service cards to draw attention to high-value items.

2. Granular Service Requirements
Since delivery is manual, every product will include a custom information capture form.

Service	Specific UI/UX Logic	Manual Fulfillment Step
Auction Legal Pack	No Progress Bar. Page shows "Processing" status only.	Admin reviews deeds and uploads final PDF to customer account.
RICS Level 2 Survey	No Date Picker. Note explains: "Our team will contact you within 24h to schedule."	Admin coordinates between the surveyor and the property owner.
Outlays	Simple line-item list of fees (Land Registry, Local Search).	Admin pays fees on client’s behalf; provides receipts post-purchase.
Professional Photography	Swipeable "Before & After" mobile gallery.	Admin assigns photographer; delivers high-res files via download link.
Property Title	"Instant Request" toggle.	Admin pulls Title from Land Registry and emails PDF manually.
Rent Increase Service	Clean, multi-step mobile form for current vs. new rent.	Admin drafts the Section 13 notice and sends to landlord.
Tenancy Agreement	Collapsible "Key Clauses" preview section.	Admin generates the legal draft and sends for digital signature.
Portal Boosting	Checkboxes for "Rightmove," "Zoopla," and "Onthemarket."	Admin manually pushes the listing to the "Featured" section on portals.
3. Mobile-First Architecture
The platform must be optimized for "thumb-browsing" with high-speed performance.

Sticky Bottom CTA: On mobile product pages, the "Add to Cart" button remains stuck to the bottom of the screen.

Haptic UI Components: Buttons should have a minimum height of 54px and use 12px rounded corners for a modern mobile feel.

Accordion Service Details: Instead of long descriptions, use "Glass" accordions that expand to show "What's Included" and "Timescales".

One-Handed Forms: All input fields for property addresses should be large, center-aligned, and utilize Google Address Autocomplete to minimize typing.

4. Manual Delivery Workflow
Until automation is built, the Shopify backend will be used as a pseudo-CRM.

Post-Purchase Redirect: Customers are sent to a "Thank You" page with a clear 4-step checklist: Order Confirmed -> Info Verified -> Service in Progress -> Delivery Sent.

Klaviyo Sequence: * Email 1 (Instant): Order summary and property details verification.

Email 2 (24 Hours): "Meet your expert" – introducing the human handling the service.

Email 3 (Completion): Manual email from Admin containing the final documents or links.

Order Status Tags: Admin uses Shopify tags (Pending Review, In Progress, Awaiting Client, Delivered) to update the customer's account view manually.

5. Technical Stack (Shopify)
Theme: Custom-built on Shopify Online Store 2.0 (Dawn) for maximum speed.

Product Options: Globo Product Options or HulkApps to capture property addresses and custom data.

Customer Portal: Bold Memberships or a custom Liquid template to create a "My Documents" area where manual uploads appear.

Reviews: Trustpilot floating widget (as seen in the mockup) for instant mobile credibility.

6. Development Priorities (Phase 1)
Mobile UI Polish: Implement the "Homemove" scroll-reveal animations and dark-mode CSS.

Address Capture: Ensure every service card forces a property address input before checkout.

Manual Loop Test: Run a test order for a "Property Title" to ensure the Admin-to-Customer delivery flow is seamless.

PRD: Digital Property Services Platform (Shopify)
1. Project Objective
To create a high-end, mobile-optimized marketplace for property services that mimics the sleek, animated feel of Homemove while managing professional service delivery through a manual, high-touch fulfillment process.

Glassmorphism & Orbs: Use "Floating Gradient Orbs" (Coral, Blue, and Purple) with high blur (80px) and low opacity (0.05-0.15) as background decorative elements to create depth.

Micro-Animations:

Reveal-on-Scroll: Sections should use a fadeIn animation (sliding 20px upward) as the user scrolls to them.

Pulse Effects: Backgrounds should have a subtle pulseBgMist animation to feel "alive".

Typography: Utilize the Poppins font family for a clean, modern, and professional aesthetic.

3. Mobile-First UX Requirements
The site must be "thumb-friendly" and optimized for high-speed mobile browsing:

Sticky Mobile Navigation: A transparent header that becomes opaque on scroll, containing only the Logo, a Search Icon, and a Hamburger Menu.

Vertical Service "Stacks": On mobile, services should not be in a grid but in large, clickable cards with a 1:1 aspect ratio and vibrant neon borders.

The "Bottom Sheet" Cart: Instead of a full-page cart, use a sliding "bottom sheet" for quick service additions, minimizing friction for mobile users.

Haptic-Style Buttons: Large, full-width "Buy Now" buttons (minimum height 54px) with rounded corners and high-contrast gradients.

4. Manual Fulfillment & Operational Workflow
Since delivery is not automated, the Shopify backend must be configured to capture data at the point of sale and manage the fulfillment pipeline manually.

A. The "Information Capture" Phase (Product Page)
Because you need to perform the service manually, you must collect specific property data before the customer checks out.

Tool: Use a "Product Options" app (e.g., Globo or HulkApps).

Fields required per service:

Full Property Address (Google Maps Autocomplete integration).

Access Details (e.g., "Is there a key safe?", "Tenant contact name").

Urgency Level (Dropdown: Standard vs. Fast Track).

B. Post-Purchase Workflow (The Manual Loop)
Order Received: Customer receives a "Welcome & Next Steps" email (via Klaviyo) explaining that an expert is now reviewing their case.

Internal Processing: Admin receives the order with all custom property details.

Manual Updates: Admin uses Shopify "Order Tags" (e.g., In-Progress, Awaiting-Surveyor, Drafting-Pack) to track status.

Delivery: Once the service (e.g., Auction Legal Pack) is ready, the Admin manually uploads the PDF/Link to the customer's "Order History" and sends a "Your Service is Ready" notification.

5. Granular Service Breakdown
Service	Specific Mobile UI Requirement	Manual Fulfillment Task
Auction Legal Pack	Progress bar showing "Drafting -> Legal Review -> Done".	Solicitor/Admin compiles deeds and legal docs into a single PDF.
RICS Level 2 Survey	Calendar date picker (optimized for mobile touch).	Admin coordinates with the surveyor and property occupier for access.
Outlays	Simple "Add to Cart" with clear cost breakdown list.	Admin verifies local authority fees and pays them on behalf of the client.
Photography	Image gallery showing "Before vs. After" (Swipeable slider).	Admin books a photographer; manual upload of edited photos via Dropbox link.
Property Title	"Instant Request" button.	Admin manually pulls the title from the Land Registry.
Rent Increase / Tenancy	Step-by-step form (One question per screen).	Admin drafts the legal notice and sends for digital signature.
Portal Boosting	"Select Platform" toggles (Rightmove/Zoopla).	Admin manually applies the "Featured" or "Premium" credit on the portal.
6. Technical Stack Recommendations
E-commerce Engine: Shopify (using a highly customized Dawn theme).

Data Capture: Globo Product Options (to collect property addresses).

Customer Communication: Klaviyo (to manage the manual delivery updates and "thank you" flows).

Mobile Performance: Cloudflare for image optimization and Lazy Loading for the "Homemove" animations.

7. Immediate Next Steps
Select a Lead Developer: Someone proficient in "Liquid" (Shopify's language) and GSAP/Framer Motion for the animations.

Map out the "Welcome Email": Draft the copy for the email customers get immediately after paying to manage their expectations regarding manual delivery times.

Setup Property Form: Define exactly what information is needed for the "Auction Legal Pack" versus a "Photography Service."

Rather than tackle this all at once, I should build it methodically, starting with the core directories and key files that make the theme functional. Building a Shopify Online Store 2.0 theme forked from Dawn for "Buy Property Services" - a UK property services marketplace.

name: Buy Property Services Shopify Theme
description: Full Shopify Dawn-forked theme for UK property services marketplace - 8 services, manual fulfillment, green/earth color palette
type: project
---

Building a Shopify Online Store 2.0 theme forked from Dawn for "Buy Property Services" - a UK property services marketplace.

**Services:** Auction Legal Pack, RICS Level 2 Survey, Outlays, Professional Photography, Property Title, Rent Increase, Tenancy Agreement, Portal Boosting.

**Color palette (from mockup):** Dark forest green, orange/amber accents, olive/lime green, cream/beige backgrounds. NOT the dark navy/cyan from the Gemini PRD.

**Key constraints:**
- Manual fulfillment only (no automated delivery)
- No progress bar for Auction Pack
- No date picker for RICS Survey ("team contacts within 24h")
- Mobile-first, thumb-friendly
- Scroll-reveal animations, glassmorphism-style cards

**Why:** User is a beginner building this as a property services business. Code goes to GitHub for Shopify to pull.

**How to apply:** Build with clean, well-commented Liquid code. Prioritize simplicity and teachability. Follow Dawn patterns closely for maintainability.

I am making a full shopify website forking the dawn theme - this is the PRD so far from Gemini Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlines the development of a high-end, Shopify-based platform for property services. Based on your provided mockups and the aesthetic of Homemove, the site will move away from a traditional "e-commerce store" look toward a "SaaS-style Digital Agency" feel.

PRD: Modern Property Services Marketplace (Shopify)
1. Project Overview
Objective: To provide a seamless, high-conversion platform for landlords and sellers to purchase legal, marketing, and survey services.

Target Audience: DIY Landlords, Property Investors, and Homeowners looking for "unbundled" estate agency services.

Visual Identity: Dark-mode aesthetic with high-contrast neon accents (Cyan & Purple), glassmorphism, and smooth scroll animations.

2. Visual & Aesthetic Requirements
Based on your mockups and the Homemove reference, the UI must follow these strict guidelines:

Color Palette:

Primary Background: Deep Navy/Charcoal (#0B1120).

Accents: Electric Cyan (#00F2FF) for primary CTAs and Purple/Magenta (#7000FF) for secondary highlights/gradients.

Surface Colors: Semi-transparent "Glass" cards (Background blur: 10px).

Typography: Bold sans-serif (e.g., Poppins or Inter).

Key "Homemove" Features to Replicate:

Reveal-on-scroll animations: Elements fading and sliding upward as the user scrolls.

Pulse Effects: Subtle glowing borders on high-value "Buy Now" cards.

Interactive Hover States: Cards that lift or glow when hovered.

3. Detailed Service Catalog (Product Logic)
Since Shopify is built for physical goods, each property service requires a custom Metadata Schema to collect property details.

Service	Requirement Logic	Key Features
RICS Level 2 Survey	Address collection + Scheduling	Calendar integration (e.g., Calendly or Tipo app).
Auction Legal Pack	Document intensive	File upload field for current deeds/titles.
Property Title	Instant digital delivery	Automated PDF delivery via Digital Downloads app.
Professional Photography	Field Service	Zip code validator to ensure coverage before checkout.
Rent Increase / Tenancy	Legal Compliance	Form-fill logic to generate the specific notice/agreement.
Portal Boosting	Variable Duration	Options for 7-day, 14-day, or "Until Sold" boosts.
4. Functional Requirements & User Flow
A. The "High-Conversion" Homepage
Hero Section: A "Choose your path" interactive toggle: I am Selling vs. I am Renting.

Trust Bar: Floating Trustpilot / Google Reviews widget (as seen in your WhatsApp mockup).

Service Grid: Use the "Glassmorphism" card style. Each card shows:

Service Icon (Custom neon line-art).

"Starting from £X" price tag.

"Add to Bundle" button (to encourage multi-service purchases).

B. Custom Product Pages (PDP)
Standard Shopify templates won't work here. We need:

Property Address Picker: A Google Maps API integrated field on the product page to verify the property address before adding to cart.

Dynamic Pricing: E.g., Survey prices that adjust based on the number of bedrooms or property value.

Process Timeline: A visual "Step 1, 2, 3" showing what happens after they pay (e.g., 1. Order Placed -> 2. Agent Assigned -> 3. Delivery in 48h).

C. The Checkout & Post-Purchase
Upsell Logic: If "Professional Photography" is in the cart, trigger a pop-up for "Portal Boosting" at a 10% discount.

Customer Dashboard: A custom "Order Status" page where users can download their RICS reports or Title Deeds once uploaded by your team.

5. Technical Stack (Shopify Ecosystem)
Theme Foundation: Dawn (for speed) but heavily customized with Liquid/CSS for the dark-mode aesthetic.

Essential Apps:

Matrixify: For managing complex service data.

Globo Product Options: To add the "Property Address" and "Number of Bedrooms" fields to products.

Search & Discovery (Shopify): For the "Recommended Services" logic.

Klaviyo: For automated "Service Reminders" (e.g., "Your Tenancy Agreement expires in 30 days").

6. Development Roadmap
Phase 1: Brand & Core Architecture (Weeks 1-2)
Set up Shopify store with the dark-mode CSS framework.

Configure the "Service" product template (removing shipping/weight logic).

Phase 2: Functional Customization (Weeks 3-4)
Integrate Google Address Autocomplete.

Build the "Bundle & Save" logic for multiple property services.

Implement the "Homemove" style animations (Framer Motion or CSS Keyframes).

Phase 3: Launch & SEO (Week 5)
SEO landing pages for "Property Title Deeds UK" and "Cheap RICS Level 2 Survey."

Payment Gateway setup (Stripe/Apple Pay/Klarna).

7. Next Steps
Would you like me to generate the specific "Product Description" copy for each of these services, optimized for both SEO and the high-end tone of your site?

Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) is an evolved version of the previous draft, now specifically focused on mobile-first architecture and a manual fulfillment workflow as per your requirements.

PRD: Digital Property Services Platform (Shopify)
1. Project Objective
To create a high-end, mobile-optimized marketplace for property services that mimics the sleek, animated feel of Homemove while managing professional service delivery through a manual, high-touch fulfillment process.

2. Visual Identity & Animations (The "Homemove" Style)
To replicate the "cool" features found in your reference sites, the frontend will implement the following:

Glassmorphism & Orbs: Use "Floating Gradient Orbs" (Coral, Blue, and Purple) with high blur (80px) and low opacity (0.05-0.15) as background decorative elements to create depth.

Micro-Animations:

Reveal-on-Scroll: Sections should use a fadeIn animation (sliding 20px upward) as the user scrolls to them.

Pulse Effects: Backgrounds should have a subtle pulseBgMist animation to feel "alive".

Typography: Utilize the Poppins font family for a clean, modern, and professional aesthetic.

3. Mobile-First UX Requirements
The site must be "thumb-friendly" and optimized for high-speed mobile browsing:

Sticky Mobile Navigation: A transparent header that becomes opaque on scroll, containing only the Logo, a Search Icon, and a Hamburger Menu.

Vertical Service "Stacks": On mobile, services should not be in a grid but in large, clickable cards with a 1:1 aspect ratio and vibrant neon borders.

The "Bottom Sheet" Cart: Instead of a full-page cart, use a sliding "bottom sheet" for quick service additions, minimizing friction for mobile users.

Haptic-Style Buttons: Large, full-width "Buy Now" buttons (minimum height 54px) with rounded corners and high-contrast gradients.

4. Manual Fulfillment & Operational Workflow
Since delivery is not automated, the Shopify backend must be configured to capture data at the point of sale and manage the fulfillment pipeline manually.

A. The "Information Capture" Phase (Product Page)
Because you need to perform the service manually, you must collect specific property data before the customer checks out.

Tool: Use a "Product Options" app (e.g., Globo or HulkApps).

Fields required per service:

Full Property Address (Google Maps Autocomplete integration).

Access Details (e.g., "Is there a key safe?", "Tenant contact name").

Urgency Level (Dropdown: Standard vs. Fast Track).

B. Post-Purchase Workflow (The Manual Loop)
Order Received: Customer receives a "Welcome & Next Steps" email (via Klaviyo) explaining that an expert is now reviewing their case.

Internal Processing: Admin receives the order with all custom property details.

Manual Updates: Admin uses Shopify "Order Tags" (e.g., In-Progress, Awaiting-Surveyor, Drafting-Pack) to track status.

Delivery: Once the service (e.g., Auction Legal Pack) is ready, the Admin manually uploads the PDF/Link to the customer's "Order History" and sends a "Your Service is Ready" notification.

5. Granular Service Breakdown
Service	Specific Mobile UI Requirement	Manual Fulfillment Task
Auction Legal Pack	Progress bar showing "Drafting -> Legal Review -> Done".	Solicitor/Admin compiles deeds and legal docs into a single PDF.
RICS Level 2 Survey	Calendar date picker (optimized for mobile touch).	Admin coordinates with the surveyor and property occupier for access.
Outlays	Simple "Add to Cart" with clear cost breakdown list.	Admin verifies local authority fees and pays them on behalf of the client.
Photography	Image gallery showing "Before vs. After" (Swipeable slider).	Admin books a photographer; manual upload of edited photos via Dropbox link.
Property Title	"Instant Request" button.	Admin manually pulls the title from the Land Registry.
Rent Increase / Tenancy	Step-by-step form (One question per screen).	Admin drafts the legal notice and sends for digital signature.
Portal Boosting	"Select Platform" toggles (Rightmove/Zoopla).	Admin manually applies the "Featured" or "Premium" credit on the portal.
6. Technical Stack Recommendations
E-commerce Engine: Shopify (using a highly customized Dawn theme).

Data Capture: Globo Product Options (to collect property addresses).

Customer Communication: Klaviyo (to manage the manual delivery updates and "thank you" flows).

Mobile Performance: Cloudflare for image optimization and Lazy Loading for the "Homemove" animations.

7. Immediate Next Steps
Select a Lead Developer: Someone proficient in "Liquid" (Shopify's language) and GSAP/Framer Motion for the animations.

Map out the "Welcome Email": Draft the copy for the email customers get immediately after paying to manage their expectations regarding manual delivery times.

Setup Property Form: Define exactly what information is needed for the "Auction Legal Pack" versus a "Photography Service."

for the auction pack we wont be able to have a progress bar and they cant pick a date for rics

Gemini said
This Product Requirements Document (PRD) is built for a high-end, Shopify-based property services platform. It incorporates the "cool" features and animations from Homemove, the functional structure of Let Property, and the specific manual fulfillment constraints you requested.

PRD: Premium Property Services Marketplace
1. Visual & Design Identity (Mobile-First)
The site will use a "SaaS-style Digital Agency" aesthetic rather than a traditional e-commerce look.

A. Color Palette & UI (Based on Mockups)
Primary Background: Deep Space Navy/Black (#0B1120).

Accents: Electric Cyan (#00F2FF) for primary buttons and Neon Purple (#7000FF) for gradients and secondary highlights.

Surface: "Glassmorphism" cards with 15% opacity white backgrounds and 10px backdrop-blur.

Typography: Poppins (Semi-bold for headers, Regular for body) to match the professional yet modern feel.

B. "Homemove" Animations & Features
Reveal-on-Scroll: Elements must slide up 20px and fade in as the user scrolls, using the fadeIn keyframes found in the Homemove code.

Floating Orbs: Decorative, blurred circles (Cyan and Purple) will move slowly in the background to provide depth.

Pulse Bg Mist: Subtle background pulses on service cards to draw attention to high-value items.

2. Granular Service Requirements
Since delivery is manual, every product will include a custom information capture form.

Service	Specific UI/UX Logic	Manual Fulfillment Step
Auction Legal Pack	No Progress Bar. Page shows "Processing" status only.	Admin reviews deeds and uploads final PDF to customer account.
RICS Level 2 Survey	No Date Picker. Note explains: "Our team will contact you within 24h to schedule."	Admin coordinates between the surveyor and the property owner.
Outlays	Simple line-item list of fees (Land Registry, Local Search).	Admin pays fees on client’s behalf; provides receipts post-purchase.
Professional Photography	Swipeable "Before & After" mobile gallery.	Admin assigns photographer; delivers high-res files via download link.
Property Title	"Instant Request" toggle.	Admin pulls Title from Land Registry and emails PDF manually.
Rent Increase Service	Clean, multi-step mobile form for current vs. new rent.	Admin drafts the Section 13 notice and sends to landlord.
Tenancy Agreement	Collapsible "Key Clauses" preview section.	Admin generates the legal draft and sends for digital signature.
Portal Boosting	Checkboxes for "Rightmove," "Zoopla," and "Onthemarket."	Admin manually pushes the listing to the "Featured" section on portals.
3. Mobile-First Architecture
The platform must be optimized for "thumb-browsing" with high-speed performance.

Sticky Bottom CTA: On mobile product pages, the "Add to Cart" button remains stuck to the bottom of the screen.

Haptic UI Components: Buttons should have a minimum height of 54px and use 12px rounded corners for a modern mobile feel.

Accordion Service Details: Instead of long descriptions, use "Glass" accordions that expand to show "What's Included" and "Timescales".

One-Handed Forms: All input fields for property addresses should be large, center-aligned, and utilize Google Address Autocomplete to minimize typing.

4. Manual Delivery Workflow
Until automation is built, the Shopify backend will be used as a pseudo-CRM.

Post-Purchase Redirect: Customers are sent to a "Thank You" page with a clear 4-step checklist: Order Confirmed -> Info Verified -> Service in Progress -> Delivery Sent.

Klaviyo Sequence: * Email 1 (Instant): Order summary and property details verification.

Email 2 (24 Hours): "Meet your expert" – introducing the human handling the service.

Email 3 (Completion): Manual email from Admin containing the final documents or links.

Order Status Tags: Admin uses Shopify tags (Pending Review, In Progress, Awaiting Client, Delivered) to update the customer's account view manually.

5. Technical Stack (Shopify)
Theme: Custom-built on Shopify Online Store 2.0 (Dawn) for maximum speed.

Product Options: Globo Product Options or HulkApps to capture property addresses and custom data.

Customer Portal: Bold Memberships or a custom Liquid template to create a "My Documents" area where manual uploads appear.

Reviews: Trustpilot floating widget (as seen in the mockup) for instant mobile credibility.

6. Development Priorities (Phase 1)
Mobile UI Polish: Implement the "Homemove" scroll-reveal animations and dark-mode CSS.

Address Capture: Ensure every service card forces a property address input before checkout.

Manual Loop Test: Run a test order for a "Property Title" to ensure the Admin-to-Customer delivery flow is seamless.

As you can see it is a property services website, with 8 different services so far, I need you to work alongside me to help me fully build this shopify website as a beginner. I will attach a mockup for the colour theme I want to use. Make me a whole project folder for Buy Property Services and we need to go through and do all Shopify sections like config, layout, images, sections, snippets, templates etc

content.thinking:
The user wants me to build a full Shopify theme forked from Dawn for their "Buy Property Services" property services marketplace. Looking at the mockup image, I can see:

- Brand: "BUY PROPERTY" with a green house/checkmark logo
- Color palette: Dark forest greens, orange/amber, olive/lime green, cream/beige, tan
- NOT the dark navy/cyan/purple from the PRD text - the mockup shows earth/green tones

The PRD mentions dark mode with cyan/purple but the mockup clearly shows a different palette. I should note this discrepancy but follow the mockup since that's what they explicitly attached as their color theme.


The mockup takes priority over the PRD's color descriptions, so I'm working with the earth-toned palette: dark forest green for headers and accents, orange-amber for CTAs, olive-lime green for secondary elements, and cream-beige backgrounds with white and tan supporting colors.

Now I'm thinking through the best way to build this Shopify Dawn theme—I could clone the official repo and customize it, but since I'm familiar with the structure, I'll create it from scratch following Dawn's architecture with the standard directories for assets, config, layouts, sections, and locales. I need to modify the templates and add custom styling and scripts to complete the customization of the cloned Dawn theme.

content.text:
I see from your mockup that you want a **green/earth-tone** palette (dark forest green, orange, olive, cream) - not the dark navy/cyan from the PRD text. I'll follow the mockup.

The smartest approach: **clone the actual Dawn theme from GitHub**, then customize it for Buy Property Services. This gives us a proper, complete foundation.

Let me start by cloning Dawn and then I'll customize it in parallel batches.

input.prompt:
Create the file C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/assets/buy-property.css

This is the main custom CSS file for a Shopify Dawn theme fork called "Buy Property Services" - a UK property services marketplace.

Color palette from the mockup:
- Dark forest green: #1A4D2E (header, nav, dark backgrounds)
- Orange/amber: #E8922A (CTA buttons, price tags, accents)
- Olive/lime green: #8FB339 (secondary accents, badges)
- Cream/beige: #F5F0E1 (page backgrounds, light sections)
- Light tan: #C4B699 (subtle backgrounds, borders)
- White: #FFFFFF (card backgrounds, text on dark)
- Dark text: #1A1A1A

The CSS should include:

1. **CSS Custom Properties** at :root for all brand colors

2. **Typography** - Import Poppins from Google Fonts, apply throughout

3. **Global Overrides** for Dawn theme:
   - Override Dawn's default colors with the green/earth palette
   - Smooth scrolling
   - Better base font sizing

4. **Header Styles**:
   - Dark green (#1A4D2E) background header
   - White text/links
   - Transparent on homepage hero, solid on scroll (via JS class `.header--scrolled`)
   - Sticky header behavior
   - Mobile: hamburger menu, clean layout

5. **Hero Banner** (`.hero-banner`):
   - Full-viewport height on desktop, 70vh mobile
   - Overlay gradient from bottom
   - Large heading with text-shadow
   - "I am Selling" / "I am Renting" toggle buttons
   - Responsive typography

6. **Service Grid** (`.service-grid`):
   - CSS Grid: 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile (stacked cards)
   - Glassmorphism cards: white background with slight transparency, subtle border, box-shadow
   - Hover effects: lift (translateY -8px), enhanced shadow, green border glow
   - Each card has icon area, title, starting price, and CTA button
   - Mobile: full-width stacked cards with min-height 54px buttons

7. **Trust Bar** (`.trust-bar`):
   - Horizontal scroll on mobile
   - Star ratings in orange
   - Clean stat cards

8. **Process Timeline** (`.process-timeline`):
   - Horizontal on desktop with connecting line
   - Vertical on mobile
   - Numbered steps with green circles
   - Icons and descriptions

9. **Service Card Snippet** (`.card-service`):
   - Glassmorphism style
   - Hover pulse/glow effect
   - Price badge styling
   - "Add to Bundle" button variant

10. **Animations**:
    - `@keyframes fadeInUp` - slide up 30px and fade in
    - `@keyframes pulse-glow` - subtle border glow pulse
    - `@keyframes float-orb` - slow floating movement for decorative orbs
    - `.reveal-on-scroll` class with opacity 0 by default, animated to 1 via JS
    - Smooth transitions on all interactive elements (0.3s ease)
    - `prefers-reduced-motion` media query to disable animations

11. **Mobile-First Responsive**:
    - Base styles are mobile, scale up with min-width media queries
    - Breakpoints: 750px (tablet), 990px (desktop), 1200px (wide)
    - Sticky bottom CTA bar on mobile product pages
    - Large touch targets (min 54px height)
    - Full-width buttons on mobile

12. **Product/Service Page Styles**:
    - Accordion-style collapsible content
    - Process timeline on product page
    - Property address input styling
    - "Starting from" price display

13. **Footer**:
    - Dark green background matching header
    - White text
    - Clean column layout
    - Social icons

14. **Utility Classes**:
    - `.text-accent` (orange)
    - `.bg-cream`, `.bg-green-dark`, `.bg-white`
    - `.btn-primary` (orange bg, white text, rounded)
    - `.btn-secondary` (green outline)
    - `.glass-card` (glassmorphism)
    - `.section-padding` (responsive padding)

Make sure the CSS is well-organized with clear section comments, mobile-first, and doesn't break Dawn's existing functionality (use specific selectors, avoid overly broad resets).

Create FOUR Shopify Liquid section files for the "Buy Property Services" Dawn theme fork. These are custom sections for a UK property services marketplace.

Color palette:
- Dark forest green: #1A4D2E
- Orange/amber: #E8922A
- Olive/lime green: #8FB339
- Cream/beige: #F5F0E1
- White: #FFFFFF

Create these files:

---

**FILE 1: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/hero-banner.liquid**

A full-screen hero section with:
- Full viewport height background image (customizable via Shopify admin)
- Dark overlay gradient from bottom
- Large heading: "Professional Property Services" (editable)
- Subheading: "Expert legal, survey & marketing services for landlords and sellers" (editable)
- Two CTA toggle buttons: "I'm Selling" and "I'm Renting" that link to different collection pages (editable links)
- Trust indicators below: "500+ Properties Served", "Rated 4.9/5", "RICS Accredited"
- Mobile responsive: 70vh on mobile, centered text
- Uses the `.hero-banner` CSS classes
- Reveal-on-scroll animation class
- Schema with settings for: heading, subheading, background_image, overlay_opacity, button_1_label, button_1_link, button_2_label, button_2_link, trust_text_1, trust_text_2, trust_text_3, color_scheme

---

**FILE 2: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/service-grid.liquid**

A grid of service cards showing the 8 property services:
- Section heading: "Our Services" (editable)
- Section subheading (editable)
- Uses blocks of type "service" - each block has: title, description, price_from (text like "From £299"), icon_name (select from predefined list), link (URL)
- Cards use glassmorphism styling (`.glass-card` class)
- Each card shows: SVG icon, service title, short description, "Starting from £X" price, "Learn More" button
- Hover effects via CSS classes
- 3-column grid desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile
- Schema allows up to 12 service blocks
- Default blocks pre-populated with the 8 services: Auction Legal Pack, RICS Level 2 Survey, Outlays, Professional Photography, Property Title, Rent Increase, Tenancy Agreement, Portal Boosting
- Each default block should have realistic placeholder descriptions and prices in GBP

The icons should use simple inline SVG icons (document icon for legal, house-search for survey, coins for outlays, camera for photo, scroll for title, arrow-up for rent increase, handshake for tenancy, megaphone for portal boosting).

---

**FILE 3: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/trust-bar.liquid**

A horizontal trust/social proof bar:
- Section heading: "Trusted by Property Professionals" (editable)
- Trust stats as blocks: each with a number, label, and optional icon
- Default blocks: "500+ Services Delivered", "4.9★ Average Rating", "24hr Response Time", "RICS Accredited"
- Horizontal layout on desktop, horizontal scroll on mobile
- Star rating display
- Optional Trustpilot-style review cards
- Clean, minimal design with cream background
- Schema with settings and blocks

---

**FILE 4: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/process-timeline.liquid**

A "How It Works" section:
- Section heading: "How It Works" (editable)
- Horizontal timeline on desktop, vertical on mobile
- 4 steps as blocks, each with: step_number, title, description, icon
- Default steps:
  1. "Choose Your Service" - "Browse our range of property services and select what you need"
  2. "Provide Property Details" - "Enter your property address and any specific requirements"
  3. "Expert Processing" - "Our team of professionals handles your service with care"
  4. "Receive Your Documents" - "Get your completed documents delivered to your account"
- Connecting line between steps (horizontal desktop, vertical mobile)
- Numbered green circles for each step
- Clean, professional design
- Schema with settings and blocks

Each file must be a complete, valid Shopify Online Store 2.0 section with proper Liquid, HTML, scoped CSS (in style tags), and a valid JSON schema block. Use Shopify's section schema format with proper type definitions.

input.prompt:
Create FIVE files for the "Buy Property Services" Shopify Dawn theme fork. This is a UK property services marketplace.

---

**FILE 1: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/assets/buy-property.js**

Custom JavaScript for the theme:

1. **Scroll Reveal Animation**: IntersectionObserver that watches `.reveal-on-scroll` elements and adds `.revealed` class when they enter viewport (threshold 0.1). Should handle elements already in viewport on page load.

2. **Header Scroll Effect**: On scroll past 50px, add `.header--scrolled` class to the header element (`.section-header`). Remove when scrolled back to top.

3. **Selling/Renting Toggle**: Handle click on `.hero-toggle-btn` buttons - add `.active` class to clicked button, remove from sibling. Could show/hide different service categories later.

4. **Smooth Scroll**: For any anchor links starting with `#`, smooth scroll to target.

5. **Mobile Bottom Sheet Cart**: Simple bottom-sheet behavior for `.cart-bottom-sheet` - slide up on open, slide down on close.

6. **Service Card Hover**: Add subtle parallax tilt effect on desktop for `.glass-card` elements on mousemove (very subtle, max 2-3 degrees).

Wrap everything in a DOMContentLoaded listener. Use vanilla JS only (no jQuery). Include comments explaining each section. Use requestAnimationFrame for scroll handlers.

---

**FILE 2: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/snippets/card-service.liquid**

A reusable service card snippet that accepts these variables:
- `card_title` - Service name
- `card_description` - Short description
- `card_price` - Price text (e.g., "From £299")
- `card_icon` - Icon name string
- `card_link` - URL to link to
- `card_color_scheme` - Color scheme class

The card should render:
- A glassmorphism card (`.glass-card .card-service`)
- Icon area at top (use a simple placeholder div with the icon name)
- Title in h3
- Description paragraph
- Price badge
- "Learn More" link/button
- The whole card is clickable (wrapped in anchor)
- Hover animation classes

---

**FILE 3: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/snippets/icon-service.liquid**

A snippet that renders inline SVG icons based on a `icon_name` variable. Support these icons:
- `document` - For Auction Legal Pack (a document/paper icon)
- `house-search` - For RICS Survey (house with magnifying glass)
- `coins` - For Outlays (stack of coins)
- `camera` - For Photography (camera icon)
- `scroll` - For Property Title (scroll/certificate)
- `arrow-up` - For Rent Increase (upward arrow with pound sign)
- `handshake` - For Tenancy Agreement (handshake)
- `megaphone` - For Portal Boosting (megaphone)
- `check-circle` - Generic checkmark circle
- `star` - Star for ratings

Each SVG should be 40x40, use `currentColor` for fill/stroke, and be clean/minimal line-art style. Use a Liquid case statement to select the icon.

---

**FILE 4: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/templates/product.service.json**

A Shopify JSON template for service product pages. This template will be assigned to all 8 property services. Structure:

```json
{
  "sections": {
    "main": {
      "type": "main-product",
      "blocks": {
        "title": { "type": "title" },
        "price": { "type": "price" },
        "description": { "type": "description" },
        "quantity_selector": { "type": "quantity_selector" },
        "buy_buttons": { "type": "buy_buttons" }
      },
      "block_order": ["title", "price", "description", "quantity_selector", "buy_buttons"],
      "settings": {
        "enable_sticky_info": true,
        "color_scheme": "scheme-1",
        "media_size": "medium",
        "media_fit": "contain",
        "gallery_layout": "stacked",
        "hide_variants": false,
        "enable_video_looping": false,
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 12
      }
    },
    "process_timeline": {
      "type": "process-timeline",
      "settings": {
        "heading": "How This Service Works",
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 36
      }
    },
    "related_services": {
      "type": "featured-collection",
      "settings": {
        "title": "Related Services",
        "heading_size": "h2",
        "products_to_show": 4,
        "columns_desktop": 4,
        "color_scheme": "scheme-1",
        "image_ratio": "square",
        "show_secondary_image": false,
        "show_vendor": false,
        "show_rating": false,
        "columns_mobile": "2",
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 36
      }
    }
  },
  "order": ["main", "process_timeline", "related_services"]
}
```

---

**FILE 5: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/main-service-product.liquid**

A custom main product section specifically for services (NOT physical goods). Based on Dawn's main-product.liquid but simplified:

- Remove all shipping/weight/variant-heavy logic
- Keep: product title, price display, description, buy button
- Add: "What's Included" collapsible accordion
- Add: "Timescales" collapsible accordion  
- Add: "Important Information" note that says "Our team will contact you within 24 hours of purchase to begin processing your service."
- Add: Property address text input field (with label "Property Address") that gets added as a line item property
- Style the buy button as the orange CTA (.btn-primary)
- Include the sticky bottom CTA on mobile
- Keep Shopify's product form functionality intact for cart/checkout to work

Schema should support blocks for: title, price, description, buy_buttons, collapsible_tab (with heading and content settings), property_address_input.

Make sure the product form uses proper Shopify Liquid (product form tag, variant selector if needed, add to cart). The property address should be captured as a cart line item property so it appears in the order.

Write all 5 files with complete, production-ready code.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Create FIVE files for the "Buy Property Services" Shopify Dawn theme fork. This is a UK property services marketplace.

---

**FILE 1: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/assets/buy-property.js**

Custom JavaScript for the theme:

1. **Scroll Reveal Animation**: IntersectionObserver that watches `.reveal-on-scroll` elements and adds `.revealed` class when they enter viewport (threshold 0.1). Should handle elements already in viewport on page load.

2. **Header Scroll Effect**: On scroll past 50px, add `.header--scrolled` class to the header element (`.section-header`). Remove when scrolled back to top.

3. **Selling/Renting Toggle**: Handle click on `.hero-toggle-btn` buttons - add `.active` class to clicked button, remove from sibling. Could show/hide different service categories later.

4. **Smooth Scroll**: For any anchor links starting with `#`, smooth scroll to target.

5. **Mobile Bottom Sheet Cart**: Simple bottom-sheet behavior for `.cart-bottom-sheet` - slide up on open, slide down on close.

6. **Service Card Hover**: Add subtle parallax tilt effect on desktop for `.glass-card` elements on mousemove (very subtle, max 2-3 degrees).

Wrap everything in a DOMContentLoaded listener. Use vanilla JS only (no jQuery). Include comments explaining each section. Use requestAnimationFrame for scroll handlers.

---

**FILE 2: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/snippets/card-service.liquid**

A reusable service card snippet that accepts these variables:
- `card_title` - Service name
- `card_description` - Short description
- `card_price` - Price text (e.g., "From £299")
- `card_icon` - Icon name string
- `card_link` - URL to link to
- `card_color_scheme` - Color scheme class

The card should render:
- A glassmorphism card (`.glass-card .card-service`)
- Icon area at top (use a simple placeholder div with the icon name)
- Title in h3
- Description paragraph
- Price badge
- "Learn More" link/button
- The whole card is clickable (wrapped in anchor)
- Hover animation classes

---

**FILE 3: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/snippets/icon-service.liquid**

A snippet that renders inline SVG icons based on a `icon_name` variable. Support these icons:
- `document` - For Auction Legal Pack (a document/paper icon)
- `house-search` - For RICS Survey (house with magnifying glass)
- `coins` - For Outlays (stack of coins)
- `camera` - For Photography (camera icon)
- `scroll` - For Property Title (scroll/certificate)
- `arrow-up` - For Rent Increase (upward arrow with pound sign)
- `handshake` - For Tenancy Agreement (handshake)
- `megaphone` - For Portal Boosting (megaphone)
- `check-circle` - Generic checkmark circle
- `star` - Star for ratings

Each SVG should be 40x40, use `currentColor` for fill/stroke, and be clean/minimal line-art style. Use a Liquid case statement to select the icon.

---

**FILE 4: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/templates/product.service.json**

A Shopify JSON template for service product pages. This template will be assigned to all 8 property services. Structure:

```json
{
  "sections": {
    "main": {
      "type": "main-product",
      "blocks": {
        "title": { "type": "title" },
        "price": { "type": "price" },
        "description": { "type": "description" },
        "quantity_selector": { "type": "quantity_selector" },
        "buy_buttons": { "type": "buy_buttons" }
      },
      "block_order": ["title", "price", "description", "quantity_selector", "buy_buttons"],
      "settings": {
        "enable_sticky_info": true,
        "color_scheme": "scheme-1",
        "media_size": "medium",
        "media_fit": "contain",
        "gallery_layout": "stacked",
        "hide_variants": false,
        "enable_video_looping": false,
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 12
      }
    },
    "process_timeline": {
      "type": "process-timeline",
      "settings": {
        "heading": "How This Service Works",
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 36
      }
    },
    "related_services": {
      "type": "featured-collection",
      "settings": {
        "title": "Related Services",
        "heading_size": "h2",
        "products_to_show": 4,
        "columns_desktop": 4,
        "color_scheme": "scheme-1",
        "image_ratio": "square",
        "show_secondary_image": false,
        "show_vendor": false,
        "show_rating": false,
        "columns_mobile": "2",
        "padding_top": 36,
        "padding_bottom": 36
      }
    }
  },
  "order": ["main", "process_timeline", "related_services"]
}
```

---

**FILE 5: C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/sections/main-service-product.liquid**

A custom main product section specifically for services (NOT physical goods). Based on Dawn's main-product.liquid but simplified:

- Remove all shipping/weight/variant-heavy logic
- Keep: product title, price display, description, buy button
- Add: "What's Included" collapsible accordion
- Add: "Timescales" collapsible accordion  
- Add: "Important Information" note that says "Our team will contact you within 24 hours of purchase to begin processing your service."
- Add: Property address text input field (with label "Property Address") that gets added as a line item property
- Style the buy button as the orange CTA (.btn-primary)
- Include the sticky bottom CTA on mobile
- Keep Shopify's product form functionality intact for cart/checkout to work

Schema should support blocks for: title, price, description, buy_buttons, collapsible_tab (with heading and content settings), property_address_input.

Make sure the product form uses proper Shopify Liquid (product form tag, variant selector if needed, add to cart). The property address should be captured as a cart line item property so it appears in the order.

Write all 5 files with complete, production-ready code.

content.thinking:
Good, I've launched 3 agents in the background:
1. Custom CSS (buy-property.css)
2. Custom sections (hero-banner, service-grid, trust-bar, process-timeline)
3. JS, snippets, and templates (buy-property.js, card-service.liquid, icon-service.liquid, product.service.json, main-service-product.liquid)

Now while those are running, I'll directly handle:
- settings_data.json (update colors)
- settings_schema.json (update theme info)
- theme.liquid (add Poppins font + custom CSS/JS)


- index.json (update homepage)
- header and footer customizations

Let me tackle these now.

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Build custom CSS for theme" completed</summary>
<result>The file has been created at `C:/Users/colby/buy-property-services/assets/buy-property.css`.

Here is a summary of what the stylesheet includes, organized into 17 clearly commented sections:

1. **Google Fonts** - Poppins import (weights 300-800)
2. **CSS Custom Properties** - All brand colors with RGB variants, semantic aliases, spacing, shadows, transitions, radii
3. **Dawn Global Overrides** - Font family, background color, smooth scrolling, heading styles, safe Dawn variable overrides
4. **Header** - Dark green background, white text/links, orange hover states, transparent-to-solid hero mode via `.header--scrolled`, sticky behavior, mobile menu drawer styling
5. **Hero Banner** - 70vh mobile / 100vh desktop, gradient overlay, responsive `clamp()` typography, text-shadow, Selling/Renting toggle buttons with `.is-active` state
6. **Service Grid** - CSS Grid (1 col -> 2 col -> 3 col), glassmorphism cards, hover lift + green border glow, icon area, price, CTA, full-width mobile buttons
7. **Trust Bar** - Horizontal scroll on mobile with snap, orange star ratings, stat cards, wrapping layout on tablet+
8. **Process Timeline** - Vertical with connecting line on mobile, horizontal on desktop, numbered green circles
9. **Service Card Snippet** - Glassmorphism, pulse-glow on hover, price badge variants, Add to Bundle button
10. **Animations** - `fadeInUp`, `pulse-glow`, `float-orb`, `.reveal-on-scroll` with `.is-visible`, `prefers-reduced-motion` respect
11. **Buttons** - `.btn-primary` (orange), `.btn-secondary` (green outline), `.btn-add-bundle` (olive), full-width on mobile
12. **Product/Service Page** - Accordion component, product timeline, property address input with focus styles, "Starting from" price display
13. **Footer** - Dark green background, white text, orange hover, social icons, scoped to both custom and Dawn footer selectors
14. **Utility Classes** - `.text-accent`, `.bg-cream`, `.bg-green-dark`, `.bg-white`, `.glass-card`, `.section-padding`, `.section-heading`/`.section-subheading`
15. **Mobile Sticky Bottom CTA** - Fixed bar with price + button, visible only below 750px, body padding offset
16. **Dawn Component Overrides** - Product form button, collection cards, newsletter, pagination (scoped selectors)
17. **Wide Breakpoint (1200px+)** - Wider page container, larger grid gaps, larger hero text</result>

input.content:
# Buy Property Services - Shopify Theme

A custom Shopify Online Store 2.0 theme forked from [Dawn](https://github.com/Shopify/dawn) for **Buy Property Services** — a UK property services marketplace for landlords, sellers, and investors.

## Services

- Auction Legal Pack
- RICS Level 2 Survey
- Outlays
- Professional Photography
- Property Title
- Rent Increase (Section 13)
- Tenancy Agreement (AST)
- Portal Boosting (Rightmove, Zoopla, OnTheMarket)

## Theme Features

- **Custom color palette**: Forest green, orange/amber, olive, cream/beige
- **Poppins typography** throughout
- **Mobile-first** responsive design with 54px touch targets
- **Scroll-reveal animations** with IntersectionObserver
- **Glassmorphism service cards** with hover effects
- **Property address capture** on service product pages (line item properties)
- **Process timeline** section showing how the service works
- **Trust bar** with stats and social proof
- **Hero banner** with Selling/Renting toggle
- **Manual fulfillment workflow** — no automated delivery

## Custom Files

### Sections
- `hero-banner.liquid` — Full-screen hero with CTA toggles
- `service-grid.liquid` — 8-service grid with glassmorphism cards
- `trust-bar.liquid` — Social proof stats bar
- `process-timeline.liquid` — "How It Works" 4-step timeline
- `main-service-product.liquid` — Service product page (no shipping logic)

### Snippets
- `card-service.liquid` — Reusable service card component
- `icon-service.liquid` — 10 inline SVG service icons

### Assets
- `buy-property.css` — Full brand stylesheet (1,150+ lines)
- `buy-property.js` — Scroll animations, header effects, mobile interactions

### Templates
- `index.json` — Homepage with hero, trust bar, services, timeline, CTA
- `product.service.json` — Service product pages
- `page.about.json` — About page
- `page.contact.json` — Contact page
- `collection.json` — Services collection

## Setup

1. Push this repo to GitHub
2. In Shopify Admin, go to **Online Store > Themes**
3. Click **Add theme > Connect from GitHub**
4. Select this repository
5. Create your products using the **Service** product template
6. Set product type to "Digital" or "Service" (no shipping required)

## Color Schemes

| Scheme | Background | Use |
|--------|-----------|-----|
| Scheme 1 | White (#FFFFFF) | Default pages |
| Scheme 2 | Cream (#F5F0E1) | Highlighted sections |
| Scheme 3 | Forest Green (#1A4D2E) | Header, footer, hero |
| Scheme 4 | Deep Green (#0D2617) | Extra dark sections |
| Scheme 5 | Olive (#8FB339) | Badges, accents |

## Based On

[Shopify Dawn Theme](https://github.com/Shopify/dawn) v15.4.1

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Buy Property Services - Shopify Theme

A custom Shopify Online Store 2.0 theme forked from [Dawn](https://github.com/Shopify/dawn) for **Buy Property Services** — a UK property services marketplace for landlords, sellers, and investors.

## Services

- Auction Legal Pack
- RICS Level 2 Survey
- Outlays
- Professional Photography
- Property Title
- Rent Increase (Section 13)
- Tenancy Agreement (AST)
- Portal Boosting (Rightmove, Zoopla, OnTheMarket)

## Theme Features

- **Custom color palette**: Forest green, orange/amber, olive, cream/beige
- **Poppins typography** throughout
- **Mobile-first** responsive design with 54px touch targets
- **Scroll-reveal animations** with IntersectionObserver
- **Glassmorphism service cards** with hover effects
- **Property address capture** on service product pages (line item properties)
- **Process timeline** section showing how the service works
- **Trust bar** with stats and social proof
- **Hero banner** with Selling/Renting toggle
- **Manual fulfillment workflow** — no automated delivery

## Custom Files

### Sections
- `hero-banner.liquid` — Full-screen hero with CTA toggles
- `service-grid.liquid` — 8-service grid with glassmorphism cards
- `trust-bar.liquid` — Social proof stats bar
- `process-timeline.liquid` — "How It Works" 4-step timeline
- `main-service-product.liquid` — Service product page (no shipping logic)

### Snippets
- `card-service.liquid` — Reusable service card component
- `icon-service.liquid` — 10 inline SVG service icons

### Assets
- `buy-property.css` — Full brand stylesheet (1,150+ lines)
- `buy-property.js` — Scroll animations, header effects, mobile interactions

### Templates
- `index.json` — Homepage with hero, trust bar, services, timeline, CTA
- `product.service.json` — Service product pages
- `page.about.json` — About page
- `page.contact.json` — Contact page
- `collection.json` — Services collection

## Setup

1. Push this repo to GitHub
2. In Shopify Admin, go to **Online Store > Themes**
3. Click **Add theme > Connect from GitHub**
4. Select this repository
5. Create your products using the **Service** product template
6. Set product type to "Digital" or "Service" (no shipping required)

## Color Schemes

| Scheme | Background | Use |
|--------|-----------|-----|
| Scheme 1 | White (#FFFFFF) | Default pages |
| Scheme 2 | Cream (#F5F0E1) | Highlighted sections |
| Scheme 3 | Forest Green (#1A4D2E) | Header, footer, hero |
| Scheme 4 | Deep Green (#0D2617) | Extra dark sections |
| Scheme 5 | Olive (#8FB339) | Badges, accents |

## Based On

[Shopify Dawn Theme](https://github.com/Shopify/dawn) v15.4.1

content.text:
All updated. Your 8 products are now named exactly:

1. **Auction Legal Pack**
2. **RICS Level 2 Survey**
3. **Outlays**
4. **Professional Photography Service**
5. **Property Title**
6. **Rent Increase Service**
7. **Tenancy Agreement**
8. **Boosting List for Rightmove/Zoopla/Onthemarket**

These names are reflected in the homepage service grid (`index.json`) and the service-grid section defaults (`service-grid.liquid`). When you create these as products in Shopify Admin, use these exact names and assign them the **Service** product template (`product.service.json`).

Want to push this to GitHub now, or would you like to review/tweak anything first?

content.text:
Pushed successfully! Your theme is live on GitHub at **https://github.com/colbybc/buy-property-services**.

### To connect it to Shopify:

1. Go to **Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes**
2. Click **Add theme → Connect from GitHub**
3. Select the `buy-property-services` repo
4. Once connected, click **Customize** to start adding your logo, hero image, and tweaking content

Want to start creating the 8 products next, or work on something else?
yes do the 8 products

content.thinking:
The user wants me to create the 8 products in Shopify. But I can't directly create products in Shopify through the admin API without access to their store. I need to think about what I can actually do here.

Options:
1. Create a CSV file that can be imported via Shopify's built-in product importer
2. Create a Matrixify import file
3. Guide them through manual creation
4. Use Shopify CLI if they have it installed

The simplest approach that actually works is creating a Shopify-compatible CSV file for product import. Shopify has a standard CSV format for importing products.


I'll build a CSV with all 8 products formatted for Shopify's import system, making sure to configure the columns properly for digital services—setting inventory to zero, disabling tracking, and using the continue policy so they don't go out of stock. Now I'm finalizing the service pricing with Boosting List at £99, then I'll generate the CSV with detailed product descriptions for each service.

content.text: I can't create products directly in your Shopify admin, but I'll create a CSV import file with all 8 products fully set up - descriptions, prices, SEO, and the correct template. You just import it.

Complete Legal Documentation for Property Auctions

Our Auction Legal Pack provides everything you need to sell your property at auction with confidence. We compile all the required legal documents, searches, and certificates into one comprehensive pack — ready for your auction house.

What's Included

  • Official Land Registry Title Deeds & Title Plan
  • Local Authority Search
  • Environmental Search
  • Water & Drainage Search
  • Chancel Repair Liability Search
  • Property Information Form (TA6)
  • Fittings & Contents Form (TA10)
  • Leasehold Information (if applicable)
  • Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) guidance

Timescales

Once you place your order, our team will contact you within 24 hours to begin processing. A typical Auction Legal Pack takes 10–15 working days to compile, depending on local authority response times.

Important Information

This is a professional service — our experienced conveyancers handle everything on your behalf. No progress bar or automated tracking; we keep you updated via email at every stage.

Professional RICS HomeBuyer Report

A RICS Level 2 Survey (formerly the HomeBuyer Report) is the most popular survey for conventional properties in reasonable condition. Our RICS-accredited surveyors provide a thorough assessment of the property's condition.

What's Included

  • Full visual inspection of the property
  • Assessment of the property's condition using a traffic-light rating system
  • Identification of significant defects and potential issues
  • Advice on urgent repairs and ongoing maintenance
  • Damp and timber assessment
  • Roof, walls, windows, and doors inspection
  • Services assessment (electrics, gas, plumbing)
  • Insurance rebuild cost valuation

Timescales

Our team will contact you within 24 hours of your order to schedule the survey at a time that works for you and the property occupier. The report is typically delivered within 3–5 working days of the inspection.

Important Information

All surveys are conducted by RICS-accredited surveyors. We do not offer an online date picker — our team personally coordinates the best time for the inspection to ensure thorough access to the property.

Disbursement Costs Handled For You

Outlays cover the essential third-party fees required during the conveyancing process. Rather than juggling multiple payments to different authorities, we handle all disbursements on your behalf — one simple payment covers everything.

What's Included

  • Local Authority Search fees
  • Environmental Search fees
  • Water & Drainage Search fees
  • Land Registry fees
  • Chancel Repair Search
  • Bankruptcy Search (where required)
  • Land Charges Search
  • All administration and processing fees

Timescales

Once payment is received, we submit all searches immediately. Local authority searches typically take 5–10 working days, though this varies by council. We provide receipts and confirmations for all disbursements made on your behalf.

Important Information

Outlay costs can vary depending on the local authority area and property type. The price shown is a standard estimate — we will confirm exact costs before proceeding and notify you of any significant variations.

Official Land Registry Title Documents

Need to know who owns a property, its boundaries, or any charges against it? We obtain official Land Registry title documents for any registered property in England and Wales — quickly and hassle-free.

What's Included

  • Official Title Register (ownership details, charges, restrictions)
  • Title Plan (property boundaries)

Legally Compliant Rent Increase Notices

Increasing rent on a periodic tenancy requires a properly drafted and served Section 13 notice. Get it wrong and the increase is invalid. Our team ensures your rent increase is legally watertight and correctly served.

What's Included

  • Assessment of your current tenancy type and terms
  • Market rent research for your area
  • Professionally drafted Section 13 notice
  • Guidance on correct service method and timing
  • Advice on tenant rights and the tribunal process
  • Digital copy of the completed notice for your records

Timescales

Once you provide your tenancy details and current rent information, we typically draft and deliver your Section 13 notice within 2–3 working days.

Important Information

A Section 13 notice must give the tenant at least one month's notice (for monthly tenancies) and can only take effect on a rent payment date. We will advise you on the correct timing to ensure compliance with the Housing Act 1988.

  • Deposit protection guidance
  • Right to Rent checklist
  • Digital copy ready for signing

Our team will contact you within 24 hours of your order to schedule the survey at a time that works for you and the property occupier. The report is typically delivered within 3–5 working days of the inspection.

Important Information

All surveys are conducted by RICS-accredited surveyors. We do not offer an online date picker — our team personally coordinates the best time for the inspection to ensure thorough access to the property.

Accredited surveyors. Book from £399.,service outlays,Outlays,"

Disbursement Costs Handled For You

Outlays cover the essential third-party fees required during the conveyancing process. Rather than juggling multiple payments to different authorities, we handle all disbursements on your behalf — one simple payment covers everything.

What's Included

  • Local Authority Search fees
  • Environmental Search fees
  • Water & Drainage Search fees
  • Land Registry fees
  • Chancel Repair Search
  • Bankruptcy Search (where required)
  • Land Charges Search
  • All administration and processing fees

Timescales

Once payment is received, we submit all searches immediately. Local authority searches typically take 5–10 working days, though this varies by council. We provide receipts and confirmations for all disbursements made on your behalf.

Important Information

Outlay costs can vary depending on the local authority area and property type. The price shown is a standard estimate — we will confirm exact costs before proceeding and notify you of any significant variations.

",Buy Property Services,Service,"outlays, disbursements, search fees, conveyancing costs, land registry",TRUE,Title,Default Title,BPS-OUT-001,0,,continue,manual,150.00,,FALSE,TRUE,,Property Outlays & Disbursements | Buy Property Services,We handle all conveyancing outlays and disbursement fees on your behalf. Simple one-payment solution from £150.,service professional-photography-service,Professional Photography Service,"

High-Quality Property Photography

First impressions matter. Our professional photographers capture your property at its absolute best with high-resolution images optimised for property portals, brochures, and marketing materials.

What's Included

  • Professional photographer visit to your property
  • Wide-angle interior photography of all key rooms
  • Exterior and kerb appeal shots
  • Garden and outdoor space photography
  • Professional editing and colour correction
  • High-resolution images for print and web
  • Portal-optimised images (Rightmove, Zoopla, OnTheMarket)
  • Delivered via secure download link

Timescales

After ordering, our team will contact you within 24 hours to schedule the photography visit. Edited images are typically delivered within 48 hours of the shoot via a secure download link.

Important Information

We recommend preparing your property before the shoot — decluttering, cleaning, and ensuring good lighting makes a significant difference. Our photographer will advise on the day. Coverage area applies; we will confirm availability for your postcode.

High-res portal-ready images from £149.,service property-title,Property Title,"

Official Land Registry Title Documents

Need to know who owns a property, its boundaries, or any charges against it? We obtain official Land Registry title documents for any registered property in England and Wales — quickly and hassle-free.

What's Included

  • Official Title Register (ownership details, charges, restrictions)
  • Title Plan (property boundaries)
  • Delivered as official PDF documents
  • Valid for legal and professional use

Timescales

We manually retrieve your title documents from the Land Registry. Most requests are completed and delivered to your email within 1–2 working days.

Important Information

This service covers registered properties in England and Wales only. If a property is unregistered, we will notify you and advise on next steps. The title documents are official Land Registry copies suitable for legal proceedings, mortgage applications, and conveyancing.

",Buy Property Services,Service,"property title, land registry, title deeds, title plan, property ownership",TRUE,Title,Default Title,BPS-PTL-001,0,,continue,manual,30.00,,FALSE,TRUE,,Property Title Deeds | Buy Property Services,Official Land Registry title deeds and title plans for any UK property. Fast delivery from just £30.,service rent-increase-service,Rent Increase Service,"

Legally Compliant Rent Increase Notices

Increasing rent on a periodic tenancy requires a properly drafted and served Section 13 notice. Get it wrong and the increase is invalid. Our team ensures your rent increase is legally watertight and correctly served.

What's Included

  • Assessment of your current tenancy type and terms
  • Market rent research for your area
  • Professionally drafted Section 13 notice
  • Guidance on correct service method and timing
  • Advice on tenant rights and the tribunal process
  • Digital copy of the completed notice for your records

Timescales

Once you provide your tenancy details and current rent information, we typically draft and deliver your Section 13 notice within 2–3 working days.

Important Information

A Section 13 notice must give the tenant at least one month's notice (for monthly tenancies) and can only take effect on a rent payment date. We will advise you on the correct timing to ensure compliance with the Housing Act 1988.

",Buy Property Services,Service,"rent increase, section 13, landlord, tenancy, rent review",TRUE,Title,Default Title,BPS-RIS-001,0,,continue,manual,49.00,,FALSE,TRUE,,Rent Increase Service | Section 13 Notice | Buy Property Services,Legally compliant Section 13 rent increase notices for UK landlords. Professionally Drafted Tenancy Agreements

A solid tenancy agreement protects both landlord and tenant. Our professionally drafted Assured Shorthold Tenancy (AST) agreements are fully compliant with current UK housing legislation and tailored to your specific property and circumstances.

What's Included

  • Professionally drafted AST agreement
  • Compliant with the Housing Act 1988 (as amended)
  • Tailored clauses for your property type (house, flat, HMO)
  • Break clause options
  • Pet and smoking clauses (if required)
  • Deposit protection guidance
  • Right to Rent checklist
  • Digital copy ready for signing

Timescales

After you provide your property and tenancy details, we typically deliver your drafted agreement within 3–5 working days. The agreement is sent digitally, ready for you and your tenant to sign.

Important Information

Our tenancy agreements are drafted by legal professionals with expertise in UK housing law. We ensure compliance with all current legislation including the Tenant Fees Act 2019, Deregulation Act 2015, and relevant statutory instruments.

Stand out from the crowd. Our portal boosting service gives your property premium placement and featured listing status on the UK's biggest property portals — Rightmove, Zoopla, and OnTheMarket — for maximum exposure and faster results.

What's Included
